mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, consumer spending power deteriorates and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the signal, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE crosses 6.0% for the third consecutive month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST diverge by more than 2 standard deviations, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio of the strategy falls below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production above 110 indicates robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns turn negative in >50% of observations when INDPRO exceeds 110, or the signal reverses direction during inflationary spikes (CPIAUCSL > 2.5% YoY).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 220 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >65% of months when CPIAUCSL exceeds 220, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL levels and 20d returns drops below -0.10 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms labor market tightening, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative returns when PAYEMS growth decelerates below 100k while UNRATE remains flat, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in quarterly regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly gross domestic product growth above trend reduces recession probabilities and supports equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters following positive GDPC1 revisions, or the predictive IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production below 105 signals manufacturing contraction and supply chain bottlenecks, which suppresses corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during downturns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to maintain a negative direction when paired with accommodative monetary policy (FEDFUNDS < 1.5%).
